2605.12456.title: TextSeal: A Localized LLM Watermark for Provenance & Distillation Protection
2605.12456.paper_summary: We introduce TextSeal, a state-of-the-art watermark for large language models. Building on Gumbel-max sampling, TextSeal introduces dual-key generation to restore output diversity, along with entropy-weighted scoring and multi-region localization for improved detection. It supports serving optimizations such as speculative decoding and multi-token prediction, and does not add any inference overhead. TextSeal strictly dominates baselines like SynthID-text in detection strength and is robust to dilution, maintaining confident localized detection even in heavily mixed human/AI documents. The scheme is theoretically distortion-free, and evaluation across reasoning benchmarks confirms that it preserves downstream performance; while a multilingual human evaluation (6000 A/B comparisons, 5 languages) shows no perceptible quality difference. Beyond its use for provenance detection, TextSeal is also ``radioactive'': its watermark signal transfers through model distillation, enabling detection of unauthorized use.
2605.12456.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了 TextSeal，一種先進的大型語言模型水印。基於 Gumbel-max 取樣，TextSeal 引入了雙鑰生成以恢復輸出多樣性，並結合熵加權評分和多區域定位以改善檢測。它支持投機性解碼和多標記預測等服務優化，並且不會增加任何推理開銷。TextSeal 在檢測強度上嚴格優於像 SynthID-text 的基準，並且對稀釋具有穩健性，即使在高度混合的人類/AI 文檔中也能保持自信的局部檢測。該方案在理論上是無失真的，並且在推理基準的評估中證實它保持下游性能；而一項多語言人類評估（6000 次 A/B 比較，5 種語言）顯示沒有明顯的質量差異。除了用於來源檢測之外，TextSeal 還是“放射性”的：它的水印信號會通過模型蒸餾轉移，從而實現對未經授權使用的檢測。
2605.12456.author: Tom Sander et.al.
2605.12456.authors: Tom Sander, Hongyan Chang, Tomáš Souček, Tuan Tran, Valeriu Lacatusu, Sylvestre-Alvise Rebuffi, Alexandre Mourachko, Surya Parimi, Christophe Ropers, Rashel Moritz, Vanessa Stark, Hady Elsahar, Pierre Fernandez
